summary: Set 9 Session 3 (D-2): Hard-scoped close_session --force to incident-recovery use only. Added closeout_force_used to EVENT_TYPES (deliberate frozen-enum exception, justified inline). Gated --force on AI_ROUTER_ALLOW_FORCE_CLOSE_OUT=1 and a mandatory --reason-file in close_session._validate_args; emit a loud WARNING to both outcome.messages (stdout/JSON) and the module logger (stderr); emit closeout_force_used to the events ledger with the operator's reason as a payload field. Threaded forced through _flip_state_to_closed and mark_session_complete so session-state.json carries forceClosed: true on emergency-bypass close-outs only when the bypass actually mattered (failing gates). Added forceClosed to the TS LiveSession type, fileSystem.ts loader, and a [FORCED] description badge + tooltip line in SessionSetsProvider. Updated close-out.md Section 5 (full --force entry rewrite), Section 2 flag-summary row, and Section 2 combination-rules block. Added forceClosedBadge.test.ts (4 cases: true/false/null/null-liveSession) and updated existing Python tests with a unified CLI happy-path test, lock-acquisition spy assertions, and refreshed warning-text expectations. All 676 Python tests pass; cross-provider verification VERIFIED (Round 3, GPT-5.4) after Round 1 raised four issues — two were context-gaps fixed by surfacing the loader and combination-rules block in the Round 2 prompt, two were genuine test-coverage gaps (no-lock-acquired spy + unified CLI happy-path test) fixed in Round 2 with the spy assertion further refined in Round 3.

reason.specifics: Session 4 is optional — F-1 (close-out trigger failure failure-injection scenario), F-2 (heartbeat alerter module), D-4 (widen failure-injection trace coverage). None block completion of the set; the operator may skip directly to Session 5 (re-audit). If Session 4 runs, surface spans test-infrastructure + new module work (concurrency-adjacent) where Opus high-effort matches the stakes; if Session 4 is skipped, the recommendation re-targets to Session 5 with the same orchestrator.
